title: New Balance Women's Fresh Foam Arishi Trail V1 Trail Running Shoe
description: Hit the trails in comfort and style with the Fresh Foam Arishi Trail running shoes from New Balance. These comfortable running shoes are built tough with performance features designed to help keep your feet safe and supported. Toe Protect technology helps shield your feet from rocks, roots and debris as you tackle new terrain, and a durable AT Tread dual lug outsole provides versatile traction for both on- and off-road adventures. These trail shoes are crafted with textile and synthetic uppers that feature classic New Balance branding for a fresh, sporty look. No-sew upper construction adds to the sleek fit and feel that you'd expect from these lightweight running shoes. Underfoot, you’re treated to super soft comfort with Fresh Foam midsole technology that is precision-engineered to deliver an ultra-cushioned, lightweight ride. An EVA foam insert adds even more cushioned comfort to help fuel your runs. Wherever your trail takes you, arrive in comfort with these sleek, stylish and supportive running shoes.
features: Imported
Rubber sole
Fresh Foam midsole cushioning is precision engineered to deliver an ultra-cushioned, lightweight ride
GORE-TEX waterproof fabric protects feet from wind, rain and water without sacrificing breathability
AT Tread outsole provides versatile traction during both on and off-road activities
Toe Protect technology helps protect feet from rocks, roots and debris
Upper features no-sew construction for a sleek fit and feel

reviews.title: Too wide, too stretchy...
reviews.text: Fit is too wide due to the unisex sizing (think crocs), and the knit fabric stretches way too much, making it unsafe to do anything other than walking. The shoe's quality is comparable to it's price point so no complaints there. They are just not form fitting, so looks like a very loose floppy sock. Ended up going with a higher end knit sneaker. Would not buy or recommend.

description:
High stability road running shoe, accomidates orthotics & overpronation
Saucony is among the most respected names in running shoes. We offer a wide range of running and walking shoes, each with the Saucony trademark fit, feel and performance. We've spent years studying the biomechanics of top athletes. Our goal? To develop creatively engineered systems that maximize your performance in your specific activity, allowing you to focus on the activity instead of the equipment. From our studies have come many innovative Saucony concepts. Advanced technologies-like Grid, the first sole-based stability and cushioning system--provide an advantage to athletes of all types.

reviews.title: Below Average
reviews.text:
As I took the shoes out of the packaging and examined them, I started laughing. The shoes are advertised on the product page as walking shoes. The label on the front of the tongue says, “Proefssional Sakteobard Shoes”... and yes, the words "professional" and “skateboard” were misspelled. Perhaps this company also makes skateboard shoes and just sewed on the wrong label, but we all got a big chuckle out of it.

At this price point, I didn’t expect much from these shoes. I wear a seven and the shoes fit fine. They are OK to wear around the house, but below average in comfort. The insole is rough and hard, and the soles are very squeaky as you walk.

With the new soft interior and a refined mesh upper, practically vanishes on the foot mainly thanks to the strategic position of stretch and Structure. Crash Pad segmented along the entire length of the foot that sustains the foot every time it touches the ground and offers soft transitions and the BioMoGo DNA and DNA Loft cushioning even more assure every step of the foot.

SMOOTH, STABLE RIDE: The Segmented Crash Pad is designed to adapt to each foot fall, so no matter how your foot lands, you’ll enjoy the smoothest ride possible.
SOFT, SECURE FIT: The newly engineered mesh upper practically disappears on foot with strategically placed stretch and structure where you need it most.

reviews.title: WAY too tight over the foot.  NOT for Diabetics, IMHO!
reviews.text:
I've had these on for about an hour now, so I am sure they are not returnable. That's too bad, because an hour has shown me that they are very uncomfortable. I cannot imagine who would find them wearable. Maybe someone with abnormally narrow feet.

The VERY stretchy material they are made from makes them hard to get on. You MUST use a shoehorn, and even then, it is not easy. They also - unpleasantly - squeeze the foot. After an hour, my toes are getting cold from low circulation! Seriously! Plus, the material does not breathe well, despite it's "perforated" appearance. So my feet are hot and wet.

The laces are just decoration. It's the tight material that holds them on. Plus, the artsy-craftsy arch inset in the instep is an invitation to soiling underneath it, without giving much arch support.
Could these be knock-offs? I assumed the attractive price was because they are a discontinued color. Maybe they are just fakes!.

PS THESE ARE NOT FOR DIABETICS. There is too much restriction of circulation.

I was advised to get "caged" Ultraboosts...the ones that lace normally. (The "cage" is the plastic framework for the laces.) But I liked the color of these.

Big mistake.

reviews.title: Runs much smaller/shorter than the Lone Peak 6, and much stiffer than expected
reviews.text: I own a pair of Lone Peak 6 size 11 which have a little toe allowance.  Same size 11 Olympus 4 literally had my toes scrunched along the front of the shoe!  May have to go one whole size up for proper fitting.  They also felt a LOT stiffer than the Lone Peaks, which surprised me given they are supposed to have maximum cushioning;  the Lone Peaks actually feel more cushy to me.  Returning the 11s and mulling over whether I should bother trying a 11.5 or a 12, considering how much stiffer they felt.

reviews.title: HD user, Size 14 feet, torn knees 50+
reviews.text:
I tear up any shoes in 3-4 months and these ones hold up the best (4+months ave.)…I’ve destroyed many pairs and won’t use anything else. They are comfortable and allow me to continue working out with aches or injury (from hiking every day).
Size 14 ft
Wgt 250
Ex-Athlete with numerous knee and back injuries
Hike 6 miles per day and workout in the gym to maintain good fitness level.
Neuropathy in feet.
Compression fractures in back (old injury)

These shoes are made for Clydesdale ex-athletes like myself…I‘be been using Altra for 4 years…

reviews.title: Nautica Rocks
reviews.text: Love the comfort in the shoe and it’s pretty color my granddaughter loves them she wore for her first day of Pre-K4 and received many compliments ❤️

reviews.title: Good quality.

reviews.title: good shoes

reviews.title: Out grow
reviews.text: My grand-babies grow so fast these are perfect.  The price is right. It matches their growth.  Buying nikes it was a waste due to the price.

reviews.title: Adorable!
reviews.text: These fit perfect. Great quality. Super cute shiny color with the white. Easy to clean. 10/10

reviews.title: Coolest shoes for a great price. My son got lots of compliments
reviews.text: Coolest shoes for a great price. My son got lots of compliments. He is 2 and a true size 8, and I ordered a 9 and his toes are at the end. So they fit perfect now but not for long, so order a size up or 2!

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: Brought these as summer shoes for my son. The design is exactly the same as the photo; durable and stylish sneaker.

reviews.title: Nice shoes for reasonable price
reviews.text: Cute and ultra light. They look like lace shoes but are actually slip in which is a bonus for a 4 year old. He just loved opening these shoes when they came and put them right on!

reviews.title: good
reviews.text: like

reviews.title: The part that the laces threads through broke
reviews.text: The part that the laces threads through broke, it's like a rubber type of material and once the little holes break the laces can't stay and you can't tie your shoes. Sucks to waste money for something he used for a few weeks

title: Brooks Women's Bedlam 3 Running Shoe
features: Rubber sole
THIS WOMEN'S SHOE IS FOR: The Bedlam 3 is perfect for runners who want a shoe that helps them by giving energy back with each step and supports the way their body naturally wants to move.
ENERGIZED CUSHIONING: Built with our best energy return, the DNA AMP midsole gives you back more of the effort you put in.
SECURE, SOCK-LIKE FIT: Redesigned Fit Knit upper provides a secure, sock-like fit that moves with you.

GUIDERAILS HOLISTIC SUPPORT SYSTEM: We’ve shifted our focus beyond the feet to the most injury-prone part of a runner’s body: the knees. GuideRails support keeps you moving comfortably by keeping excess movement in check.
WEAR TESTERS SAY: “ I love the energy I have in these shoes, they are light but powerful! Traction and stability are very strong as well.”

reviews.title: Change the laces
reviews.text: These tennis shoes are very comfortable but the laces won’t stay tied.

title: WHITIN Men's Max Cushioned Running Shoes | Superior Comfort, Yet Remaining Stability
features: Ethylene Vinyl Acetate sole
Open engineered knit upper offers enhanced breathability and comfort.
Rubberized foam outsole provides long-lasting durability and adequate grip.
Internal heel counter provides lightweight response and lasting durability and support.
Lace-up closure and padded tongue provides locked-in fit and support for neutral runners.
Durable density foam midsole with a lower heel to toe drop provide lightweight and propulsive power.

reviews.title: Buy a Size Larger
reviews.text: This show has a short toe box but with plenty of heel and arch space.  I'm glad that I purchased a 10 instead of a 9.5 .  It is a perfect fit, really supports my knees. Terrific product 😊

reviews.title: Not a running shoe
reviews.text:
Surprising. This is not a road shoe by any stretch of the imagination. I've been switching from heel toe stride to mid foot striker. Not comfortable enough and minimal cushioning
This at best is for walking.
Also tight in the toe box. Kind of surprising. I will not be buying more of these
Also oddly they came in plastic wrap. No box. Cosmetic but just odd

reviews.title: Good stable comfortable shoe
reviews.text: I finally found a show that offers stability comfort and no memory foam! Considering stocking up!

reviews.title: You'll get what you pay for.
reviews.text: Updated: Over the long term, wearing them daily for work...Garbage.

reviews.title: Horrible shoe. Barbie shoes are better quality.
reviews.text: I don't like to write negative reviews but this shoe truly sucks. So cheaply made. Colour was nice though .

title: Saucony Men's Cohesion 15 Running Shoe
description: The Cohesion 15 gives you everything you need for a feel-good run—lots of VERSARUN cushioning, a smooth transition from heel to toe, and a comfortable, secure fit.
features: 100% Synthetic
Imported
Rubber sole

Cushioned comfort.VERSARUN cushioning absorbs impact and reduces pressure for comfortable strides every step of the way.Durable performance.The rubber utsole takes cues from our most trusted styles for a smooth feel that holds up to lots of miles.Lighten up.

reviews.title: Love these comfortable shoes
reviews.text: These shoes are as it is described for their comfort. It's like walking on a mattress and so much room. I ordered 8.5 wide width because I like a lot of room for my feet. I will most likely get another pair. Great for for heels with Achilles pain.

reviews.title: Good cushioning.
reviews.text: These have more support than my Asics running shoes. They fit my narrow feet quite well. I wear men's shoes due to long feet, so width can be a challenge. They're well made.

reviews.title: Great running shoes
reviews.text: Great looking shoe that fits well. Plenty of cushion and extremely light weight. I’ve been using Asics for over 5 years now and have never wanted to switch. These are comparable to both quality and performance. Overall very please. Will be using these for running as well as casual wear.

reviews.title: 5 stars
reviews.text: My son has the widest feet that I have ever seen in my life. These shoes are perfect for him. He said that they are very comfortable and they fit him true to size. He LOVES them!

reviews.title: Solid support!
reviews.text:
As these are made for speed, the weight should tell you these really are made of lightweight materials.
Are there lighter shoes out there?
Well, you're the runner, not me, but I suspect yes, probably.
I also suspect the lighter the shoe, the higher the price and the shorter the life, but what do I know......
